mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings prospects, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate amplifies consumer spending capacity and lowers discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% annually while FEDFUNDS remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 70% of observations during low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 150k monthly but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.0 million units signals a contraction in construction activity and reduced household wealth effects, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months where housing starts drop below 1.0 million.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and reduces forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising CPI and rising rates yields positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion and supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during stable labor markets.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% coincides with negative 60-day forward returns in more than 65% of quarterly observations.
